title: (Sacramento, CA) – Assemblywoman Pilar Schiavo was reappointed by Speaker Robert Rivas as Chair of the Assembly Military and Veterans Affairs Committee for the upcoming legislative
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: The Military and Veterans Affairs Committee plays a vital role in addressing the needs of California’s veterans, service members, and their families.
Assemblywoman Schiavo advocated for policies that support veterans, service members, and their families.
The legislation AB 535 ensures that veterans' disability benefits are not counted against them as income when determining eligibility for supportive, affordable, and transitional housing.
AB 1994 establishes a permanent position within the California Department of Veterans Affairs to advocate for minority and underrepresented veterans.
AB 1854 extends the time for reservists or National Guard members to apply for retroactive deferment of payment and interest on certain debts.
Assemblywoman Schiavo co-founded an organization that helped secure housing for veterans experiencing homelessness.
Assemblywoman Schiavo delivered healthcare, including reproductive healthcare, to more than one million people throughout her career.
Assemblywoman Schiavo delivered more than 50,000 meals to people in need.
